children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall contain provisions on the allowability of contractor costs. Such provisions shall define in detail and in specific terms those costs which are unallowable, in whole or in part, under covered contracts.
Membership in civic, community, and professional organizations.

Actions to influence (directly or indirectly) executive branch action on regulatory and contract matters (other than costs incurred in regard to contract proposals pursuant to solicited or unsolicited bids).
Professional and consulting services, including legal services.

children.label: A
children.text: adequate documentation with respect to such costs; and

children.label: B
children.text: the opinion of the contract auditor on the allowability of such costs.

children.heading: .—Presence of contract auditor
children.label: 2 .—Presence of contract auditor
children.text: The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall provide that, to the maximum extent practicable, the contract auditor be present at any negotiation or meeting with the contractor regarding a determination of the allowability of indirect costs of the contractor.

children.heading: .—Settlement to reflect amount of individual questioned costs
children.label: 3 .—Settlement to reflect amount of individual questioned costs
children.text: The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall require that all categories of costs designated in the report of the contract auditor as questioned with respect to a proposal for settlement be resolved in such a manner that the amount of the individual questioned costs that are paid will be reflected in the settlement.
